ddls:
CREATE TABLE "stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_code" TEXT /* Code representing the station (e.g., VVTS) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the station's location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the station's location */,
  "altitude" REAL /* Altitude of the station above sea level */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the station was installed */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of station (e.g., airport, urban, rural) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "observations" (
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each observation */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station making the observation */,
  "observation_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the observation */,
  "altitude_hPa" REAL /* Altitude in hPa at the time of observation */,
  "temperature_C" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "dew_point_C" REAL /* Dew point in degrees Celsius */,
  "relative_humidity" REAL /* Relative humidity percentage */,
  "wind_direction_deg" REAL /* Wind direction in degrees */,
  "wind_speed_m_s" REAL /* Wind speed in meters per second */,
  "visibility_km" REAL /* Visibility in kilometers */,
  "clouds" TEXT /* Cloud cover description */,
  "na_0" TEXT /* Reserved for additional information */,
  "precipitation_mm" REAL /* Precipitation in millimeters */,
  "atmospheric_pressure_hPa" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure in hPa */,
  "weather_condition" TEXT /* Weather condition (e.g., sunny, cloudy, rainy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("observation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_observations_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the weather type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather data type (e.g., Temperature, Humidity) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the type */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the type (e.g., Celsius, percentage) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cloud_cover_types" (
  "cloud_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cloud cover type */,
  "cloud_description" TEXT /* Description of the cloud cover */,
  "cloud_cover_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of cloud cover */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cloud_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the observation */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the observation was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_observation_id FOREIGN KEY ("observation_id") REFERENCES observations ("observation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather report */,
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation that generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date and time the report was generated */,
  "report_content" TEXT /* Content of the weather report */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_reports_observation_id FOREIGN KEY ("observation_id") REFERENCES observations ("observation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather model */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the forecasting model */,
  "model_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model (e.g., 0-100) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast result */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecasting model used */,
  "observation_time" TEXT /* Date and time for which the forecast is made */,
  "forecasted_temperature" REAL /* Forecasted temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "forecasted_humidity" REAL /* Forecasted humidity percentage */,
  "forecasted_precipitation" REAL /* Forecasted precipitation in millimeters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_results_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecast_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather alert */,
  "observation_id" INTEGER /* ID of the observation associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of weather alert (e.g., storm, warning) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Content of the alert message */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time the alert was issued */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_alerts_observation_id FOREIGN KEY ("observation_id") REFERENCES observations ("observation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "satellite_data" (
  "satellite_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each satellite */,
  "satellite_name" TEXT /* Name of the satellite */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data collected (e.g., image, sensor reading) */,
  "data_date" TEXT /* Date and time the data was collected */,
  "data_content" TEXT /* Content of the satellite data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("satellite_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data collected (e.g., temperature, humidity) */,
  "data_date" TEXT /* Date and time the data was collected */,
  "data_content" TEXT /* Content of the sensor data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date and time the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)
